title: Celtics extend Walsh to 3-year, $15M deal
dek: The Boston Celtics have agreed to a three-year, $15 million contract extension with forward Jordan Walsh, keeping him through the 2029-30 season. Walsh emerged as a key rotation player last season, shooting 38% from three-point range after converting at 27% in his first two years.
author: The GoCheckMySports Desk
key_fact: Jordan Walsh signed a three-year, $15 million extension with Boston, securing him through 2029-30 with the team's pickup of his $2.4 million option for 2026-27.
bottom_line: The Bottom Line: Walsh's extension anchors depth at a position the Celtics remade with their Brown trade. Watch whether his improved shooting sustainability holds under increased usage in the post-George roster construction.
body: The Boston Celtics agreed to a three-year, $15 million contract extension with forward Jordan Walsh, according to ESPN, securing him through the 2029-30 season with the club exercising his $2.4 million option for the 2026-27 campaign.

Walsh, a second-round pick in 2023, emerged as a key rotation player for the defending champions last season, averaging career highs of 5.4 points and 4.0 rebounds across 68 games, including 25 starts, per ESPN's reporting.

The forward's three-point shooting marked a significant leap. Walsh connected on 38% of his attempts from beyond the arc last season, a sharp improvement from the 27% he shot in his first two seasons, according to ESPN. At 6-foot-6, Walsh became a functional bench wing who could space the floor for Boston's core.

The extension represents roster continuity for a team navigating significant roster change this offseason. The Celtics made a franchise trade this summer, sending All-Star Jaylen Brown to the Philadelphia 76ers for Paul George, per ESPN. Retaining Walsh on long-term terms signals the organization's confidence in his ability to occupy a rotation role alongside the new configuration.
No performance bonuses or incentive details were disclosed in the reporting, and the exact timing of when the extension was formally finalized remains unspecified.
